summary: Regression run for state_code wiring on POST /api/notary/requests and the multi-state pre-seal gate on POST /api/notary/requests/{id}/complete. Built /app/backend/tests/test_multistate_preseal_regression.py with 22 pytest cases; all 22 PASSED in 200s. Validates: (a) state_code validation accepts FL/TX/NY/CA/VA, normalizes lowercase 'tx' -> 'TX', rejects 'ZZ' with 400 'Unsupported state_code', and remains backward-compatible when state_code is missing/empty; (b) /complete blocks TX/NY/CA/VA ceremonies with HTTP 412 + detail.error='preseal_gate_failed' + non-empty blocked_reasons, persists `<state>_blocked` status in DB (verified via /requests/my), NY hard-blocks document_type='will'; (c) FL ceremony end-to-end (create -> assign -> complete) still returns 200 with package_id and package_hash — the multi-state gate is correctly bypassed for FL; (d) health GET /api/ = 200, and admin/demo/FL notary/notary2 logins all succeed.
critical_code_review_comments: notary_routes.py:537 — complete_notarization signature uses `notes: str = ''` and `seal_package: bool = True` as plain query params (not Body). FastAPI will treat them as query string args; if a client posts them in the body they will be ignored. Consider Pydantic body model or Body(...) wrappers.

notary_routes.py:599-602 — the broad `except Exception` around the evaluator silently swallows evaluator errors and proceeds to seal. A bug in the evaluator (e.g. DB outage) would let a non-FL ceremony complete despite the gate. Recommend logging + 503 fail-closed, or at minimum re-raise on non-network errors.

multistate_evaluator.py:46-47 — `_check_av` reads from `fl_av_quality_reports` for all states; collection name is FL-specific and may not reflect TX/NY/CA/VA captures if they are stored elsewhere. Generic `av_quality_reports` collection or per-state aliasing is cleaner.

multistate_evaluator.py:74-90 — same FL-prefix collection reuse (`fl_retention_tags`, `fl_journal_entries`) for non-FL states; works today but is misleading and tightly couples non-FL gates to FL collections.
notary_routes.py file length is 751 lines — approaching the >700-line split threshold called out in the review guidelines.
test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com/Admin123!, demo@test.com/Demo123!, notarytest@test.com/Test123!, notary2@test.com/Notary123! — all verified working
seed_data_creation: Multiple ephemeral TEST_ prefixed notarization requests created across FL/TX/NY/CA/VA states; not cleaned up (id-namespaced TEST_ prefix). No production data modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite for state_code + multi-state evaluator lives at /app/backend/tests/test_multistate_preseal_regression.py and runs in ~3:20. It is safe to re-run on any iteration touching notary_routes.py or services/multistate_evaluator.py. The FL happy-path test will hit Hedera HCS mainnet topic 0.0.10373605 on each completion — call out budget if running repeatedly.
rca of the issue: N/A — no regressions found. state_code wiring and the multi-state pre-seal gate behave per spec; FL legacy pipeline is intact and verifiably returns a sealed package.
